qa_pairs.question: What is the general principle for how research groups are charged for core equivalents?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups are charged for the maximum number of core equivalents they take from the resources.

qa_pairs.question: What is the assumed composition of a core equivalent for charging purposes?
qa_pairs.answer: A core equivalent is assumed to be a bundle made up of 1 core and 4GB of memory.

qa_pairs.question: How is usage calculated if a research group requests more cores than memory, relative to the standard core equivalent ratio?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups using more cores than memory (above the 1 core/4GB memory ratio) will be charged based on the number of cores requested.

qa_pairs.question: If a research group requests two cores and 2GB per core (for a total of 4GB of memory), how many core equivalents will be counted for priority calculation?
qa_pairs.answer: This job request will be counted as 2 core equivalents when priority is calculated, as it requires 2 core equivalents worth of cores, despite only needing one memory bundle.

qa_pairs.question: Why would a job requesting two cores and 4GB of total memory be charged as 2 core equivalents?
qa_pairs.answer: It is charged as 2 core equivalents because the request requires 2 core equivalents worth of cores (two cores), even though the total memory (4GB) only equates to one memory bundle based on the 1 core/4GB ratio. The charge is based on the maximum resources taken.
